title: Arrow Functions
description: Write concise functions with arrow syntax and understand `this` binding.

slides.title: Function Declarations vs Expressions
Arrow Function Syntax 🏹
Implicit Return Example
slides.codeSnippet:
const greet = name => `Hello, ${name}!`;
console.log(greet('Node')); // Hello, Node!

slides.title: Lexical `this` 🔍

quizData.explanation: The braces `{}` after arrow are interpreted as the function body, not an object literal. To return an object, wrap it in parentheses: `() => ({ x: 5 })`.

slides.title: When NOT to use arrow functions 🚫
slides.content:
## Situations where arrow functions can cause trouble

* **Object methods** that need to access the object via `this` – use regular functions.
* **Constructors** – arrow functions cannot be used with `new`.
* **Event handlers** where you need dynamic `this` binding.

Otherwise, arrow functions are a great default for callbacks and short functions.
